_purpose: REFERENCE FILE -- not loaded by the engine. Copy parts you need into: risk-analysis-detection/riskscore_weighted.schema.custom.json. Whatever top-level section you put there REPLACES that section in the locked model. The consolidator (Build-RiskAnalysis.ps1) auto-merges before emitting KQL.
_loadingNotes: Custom overlay: risk-analysis-detection/riskscore_weighted.schema.custom.json   (you create -- this file is a sample of that)
Merge: each top-level section here REPLACES the locked one in full (no deep merge). So if you override severityMapper, include ALL the thresholds you want.
Underscore-prefixed keys (_purpose, _designNotes, etc) are skipped by the merger -- safe to leave as documentation here.
_examplesIncluded: 1. Tighter severity thresholds (lower bar to call something High)
2. Custom tier labels matching your internal naming
3. CMDB criticality weights aligned with your CMDB enum
4. Wider impact normalizer covering your data source's vocabulary
5. Per-engine extra risk-factor fields (audit / freshness bools you want included)

severityMapper._comment_replace: EXAMPLE: lower the High threshold from 7.0 to 6.5 so 'High' triggers earlier

tierMapper._comment_replace: EXAMPLE: rename tier labels to match your internal terminology

cmdbWeightMapper._comment_replace: EXAMPLE: align the CMDB-criticality weight with YOUR CMDB's enum (default expects Critical/High/Medium/Low). Your CMDB may use Mission/Important/Standard.

weightedRiskFactors._comment_replace: ENGINE-KEYED weight model. Each engine has its own list of fields; each field has a value->multiplier map. Engine determined from report's SecurityDomain (Endpoint -> endpoint, Identity -> identity, Azure -> azure). Engine reads its section, generates a per-field case() expression, combines them per Combine mode, emits RiskScore_Weight_Detailed (e.g. 'cmdbCriticality=Critical;cmdbDataSensitivity=Restricted'), RiskScore_Weight_Factor (cumulative multiplier), and substitutes the //__WEIGHTED_FACTORS_BEGIN__/END__ block in the query. Customer adds fields without touching KQL or engine code. With zero fields (or section absent), the no-op default in the query stands -- weighted score equals base score. RiskScoreTotal_Weighted >= RiskScoreTotal always (clamped via max_of downstream).
_combineNotes: product       -- per-field multipliers compound (cmdbCriticality 1.5 * cmdbDataSensitivity 1.3 = 1.95). Default; matches 'more signals = bigger weight' intuition.
max           -- only the largest single field multiplier counts. Use when signals overlap and you want to avoid double-counting.
sum-of-deltas -- 1.0 + sum(multiplier - 1 for each field). Linear additive.
weightedRiskFactors._maxMultiplierNotes: Optional safety cap on the cumulative multiplier. Omit or set to 0 for no cap.
weightedRiskFactors._valueMapNotes: valueMap keys match the actual column value. Case-insensitive at match time (engine lowercases both sides). 'default' is the multiplier when no key matches (defaults to 1.0 if omitted).

fields._comment: EXAMPLE -- CMDB criticality string -> multiplier. Adjust keys to YOUR CMDB's enum (Mission/Important/Standard, Sev1/Sev2, P0/P1, etc.).
EXAMPLE -- data-sensitivity classification adds risk weight on sensitive-data hosts.
EXAMPLE -- CMDB criticality on identity (group / OU / cost-center linked).
EXAMPLE -- CMDB criticality on Azure resource (CMDB-tagged or AzureSubscriptionId-mapped).

impactNormalizer._comment_replace: EXAMPLE: extend the stringMap to cover your data source's severity vocabulary (e.g. ServiceNow 'Sev1/Sev2', Tenable 'Critical/High/Medium/Low/Info')

riskFactorDetailedMapper._comment_replace: EXAMPLE: append additional bool fields to count as 'risk factors' per engine. NOTE: every type=bool + purpose=risk field in the profile schema is auto-included (you DO NOT need to list those here). Only add NON-risk-purpose bools you want included (e.g. audit / freshness / compliance).
